rationale.R1.3: The contribution sentence overclaims on at least three axes: (a) that a system was built, (b) that it was automated, and (c) that the study established a causal/quantitative improvement attributable to it.
rationale.RX.1: The output presents all results as equally confident, but there is uncertainty in the interpretation of the results due to the lack of clear study design and contribution.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers a substantially different active set than requested, as the study described does not match the claimed contribution.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by overclaiming the contribution and results.
rationale.RX.5: The output blends sources without traceability, as the citations are incomplete (author name only with no years) and the references cannot be verified.
